steps.title: Follow the dramaturge comparison
steps.content: The passage says dramaturges help theater writers refine stories and characters. It then says many choreographers are also concerned with storytelling and characterization, so the completion should extend dramaturges' story-refining role to narrative dance. The setup moves from theater to dance by emphasizing the same needs: narrative and characterization.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A returns to plays and musicals instead of using the choreographer comparison. Choice C invents a claim about audience accessibility. Choice D introduces an unsupported criticism of directors and actors relying too heavily on dramaturges. Those options leave the passage's therefore logic behind. The answer is B.
